Now that we have watches giving the Indian Standard Time the
difficulty of looking up to the sky or down on the ground is
obviated. So what you have to do to start with is to ascertain
the correct time of birth as well as the place of birth. Then have
before you Lahiri's "Tables of Ascendants" and the Ephemeris
for the year. The ascendant (lagna) and other houses (a Rasi
and a house in a horoscope need not be identical) vary according
to the latitude of the place. To calculate the ascendant you
have to convert the time in 1. S. T. to the Local Mean Time which
you can read from the last page of Lahiri's Ephemeris. For
example you find against Delhi —Latitude 28-39; Longitude
77-13; Local lime by correction to 1. S. T. minus 21 m. 8 s. and
lastly correction to Indian Sidereal Time-Hplus) 3 s. Hence
you will deduct 21 minutes 8 seconds from the 1. S. T. in order
to get the Local Mean Time (L. M. T.) at Delhi.
